steps.title: Find the y-intercept
steps.content: The y-intercept is the value of $f(t)$ when $t = 0$. Substitute: $$f(0) = 6{,}400(0.75)^{0} = 6{,}400 \times 1 = 6{,}400$$

steps.title: Interpret $t=0$
steps.content: Since $t$ counts years since the end of 2004, $t = 0$ corresponds to the end of 2004. So the y-intercept of 6,400 represents the number of emails sent at the end of 2004.

steps.title: Check the choices
steps.content:
Evaluate all choices:
  • A: The minimum estimated number of emails the organization sent to its subscribers during the $5$ years was $2,025$. Says the minimum over 5 years was 2,025. The y-intercept is an initial value, not a minimum; and 2,025 is not 6,400. Incorrect.
  • B: The minimum estimated number of emails the organization sent to its subscribers during the $5$ years was $6,400$. Says the minimum over 5 years was 6,400. The y-intercept is an initial (starting) value, not a minimum; the function decreases, so the minimum is at $t=4$, not $t=0$. Incorrect.
  • C: The estimated number of emails the organization sent to its subscribers at the end of $2004$ was $2,025$. Says the number of emails at the end of 2004 was 2,025. The correct y-intercept value is 6,400, not 2,025. Incorrect.
  • D: The estimated number of emails the organization sent to its subscribers at the end of $2004$ was $6,400$. Says the estimated number of emails at the end of 2004 was 6,400. This correctly identifies both the time ($t=0$ = end of 2004) and the value (6,400). That matches Choice D — The estimated number of emails the organization sent to its subscribers at the end of $2004$ was $6,400$..
